text_original: Under the conditions of Da Xu it will be advantageous to be firm and correct. (If its subject do not seek to) enjoy his revenues in his own family (without taking service at court), there will be good fortune. It will be advantageous for him to cross the great stream.
text_normalized: Under the conditions of Da Xu it will be advantageous to be firm and correct. (If its subject do not seek to) enjoy his revenues in his own family (without taking service at court), there will be good fortune. It will be advantageous for him to cross the great stream.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=Judgment.

text_original: The first NINE, undivided, shows its subject in a position of peril. It will be advantageous for him to stop his advance.
text_normalized: The first NINE, undivided, shows its subject in a position of peril. It will be advantageous for him to stop his advance.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=first.

text_original: The second NINE, undivided, shows a carriage with the strap under it removed.
text_normalized: The second NINE, undivided, shows a carriage with the strap under it removed.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=second.

text_original: The third NINE, undivided, shows its subject urging his way with good horses. It will be advantageous for him to realise the difficulty (of his course), and to be firm and correct, exercising himself daily in his charioteering and methods of defence; then there will be advantage in whatever direction he may advance.
text_normalized: The third NINE, undivided, shows its subject urging his way with good horses. It will be advantageous for him to realise the difficulty (of his course), and to be firm and correct, exercising himself daily in his charioteering and methods of defence; then there will be advantage in whatever direction he may advance.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=third.

text_original: The fourth six, divided, shows the young bull, (and yet) having the piece of wood over his horns. There will be great good fortune.
text_normalized: The fourth six, divided, shows the young bull, (and yet) having the piece of wood over his horns. There will be great good fortune.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=fourth.

text_original: The fifth six, divided, shows the teeth of a castrated hog. There will be good fortune.
text_normalized: The fifth six, divided, shows the teeth of a castrated hog. There will be good fortune.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=fifth.

text_original: The sixth NINE, undivided, shows its subject (as) in command of the firmament of heaven. There will be progress.
text_normalized: The sixth NINE, undivided, shows its subject (as) in command of the firmament of heaven. There will be progress.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=top.
